business.headline: Are banks doing enough to keep your money safe?Digital fraud is getting smarter as AI fuels more complex scams, but are banks ready to keep your money safe in the age of instant payments?

When risk itself begins to drift: Reading the Mythos moment for Indian financeWhy Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman raised concerns about the security of our financial systems in the wake of the emergence of Mythos, should India worry, and what must it do to secure one of the world's largest digital financial stacks?

Decoding the AI threat to banks that Nirmala Sitharaman warned aboutFinance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman met bank chiefs to discuss rising AI-linked risks in the financial system. To understand how serious the threat could become, we dug deeper into the risks banks face from AI and spoke to experts across the cybersecurity and technology sectors.

RBI cancels Paytm Payments Bank licence: What it means for Paytm usersThe RBI has cancelled Paytm Payments Bank's licence after years of restrictions and scrutiny. The big question now is how this move could affect everyday Paytm users.

The many ways motor insurance is mis-sold in IndiaMotor insurance mis-selling in India is not an exception, it is part of how the system works. What customers don't know at the time of purchase often costs them the most at the time of claim.

Reliance Q4 profit slips to Rs 16,971 crore; Rs 6 dividend announcedReliance Industries posted consolidated net profit attributable to owners of Rs 16,971 crore for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, down from Rs 19,407 crore a year earlier.

RBI cancels Paytm Payments Bank licence; to seek winding up in courtThe action marks a dramatic escalation in regulatory steps against Paytm Payments Bank, which had already faced severe business restrictions over the past two years.

Is not using your credit card doing more harm than good?For many people, the safest way to avoid debt is simple, i.e., stop using credit cards altogether. No swipes, no bills, no worries. But could this seemingly smart move actually be harming your credit score?

How this 25-year old built a Rs 20 lakh portfolio in almost 3 yearsBuilding a Rs 20 lakh portfolio usually feels like a long-term goal. But a 25-year-old techie managed to do it in just about three years. The surprising part? His salary wasn't very high, so what did he do differently?
Sensex ends 982 points lower, Nifty below 24,000; IT stocks tankSensex ends 982 points lower, Nifty below 24,000; IT stocks tank

Bernstein flags job risk, subsidy burden in open letter to PM ModiThe letter, which revisits themes first raised in 2019, comes at a time when India has moved up global GDP rankings but continues to face challenges in employment, innovation and infrastructure.

Should you pause your SIP when markets fall? Here's the truth most investors missThe moment markets dip, doubt creeps in. That steady SIP (Systematic Investment Plan) you trusted now feels risky. Should you stop and play safe for a while? The answer isn't as obvious as it seems.

Infosys grants Rs 51.75 crore ESOPs to CEO, employees still await pay hikeThe ESOP approval comes after a strong quarterly performance by the company. Infosys reported a 20.9% rise in net profit to Rs 8,501 crore in the March quarter, while revenue grew 13.4%.

Explained: How Rs 5 lakh PF withdrawal after 3 years could cost you Rs 1.5 lakhWithdrawing your PF after switching jobs may seem like an easy financial move. But what many don't realise is that an early withdrawal can come with a surprisingly high tax cost, cutting a big chunk from your savings.
Why HCLTech shares are under pressure againThe fresh decline comes just days after the company's latest quarterly results disappointed the Street and triggered a sharp sell-off in the stock.

Received money, mutual funds or shares as gifts? Don't miss these ITR rulesGifts in the form of money or investments are quite common, but they come with tax rules that are often overlooked. Knowing what to declare in your ITR can save you from unnecessary notices later.

8th Pay Commission: Key meetings to begin in Delhi from April 28, more rounds plannedIn a notice dated April 24, 2026, the Commission said it has received a large number of requests from employee unions and associations for meetings scheduled between April 28 and April 30 in Delhi.

Infosys share price crashes after Q4 results: Why stock fell 6% todayThe IT giant's share price declined 6% intraday, making it one of the biggest losers in the technology pack, as investors reacted more to future guidance than to the quarterly profit itself.

AI security risk for banks? Why has Nirmala Sitharaman raised concernsAt the centre of the issue is Anthropic's new AI model, known as Claude Mythos Preview, capable of identifying and exploiting vulnerabilities across major operating systems and web browsers when prompted.

Infosys shares down 5%, hit 52-week low: Why IT stock tumbled after Q4 resultsAt around 11:01 am, Infosys shares were trading at Rs 1,178.20 on the NSE, down 5.03% or Rs 62.40 for the day. The fall made it one of the worst performers on benchmark indices as the IT pack remained under pressure.

entertainment.headline: Rakul Preet, Jackky Bhagnani laugh off situationship tag on marriage. Watch Actor Rakul Preet Singh posted a playful Instagram Stories video with her producer-husband Jackky Bhagnani over his 'situationship' remark. The post echoed her earlier point that one line from their interview was taken out of context.
Riteish Deshmukh slams 'distorted nonsense' about Raja Shivaji Actor Riteish Deshmukh on Sunday strongly criticised what he termed as distorted nonsense about Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj.

Selena Gomez-Benny Blanco to divorce? Here's what we know about the viral claim Viral posts have claimed Selena Gomez and Benny Blanco are heading for a divorce. Read on to know what led to the speculation about the couple, who got married on September 27, 2025.

Varun Dhawan shares 'work nobody sees' behind Wow song, celebrities react Actor Varun Dhawan shared a behind-the-scenes rehearsal video from the Wow song, which features in his upcoming film, Hai Jawaani Toh Ishq Hona Hai. The clip drew reactions from actors Jacqueline Fernandez, Maniesh Paul and choreographer Bosco Martis.

Karan Johar calls out testosterone-heavy films and Bollywood's alpha obsession Karan Johar criticised Hindi cinema's current tilt towards hyper-masculine, testosterone-heavy storytelling. He said the trend reflects Bollywood's herd mentality and recalled backlash to a scene in Rocky Aur Rani Kii Prem Kahaani.

The Devil Wears Prada 2 advance bookings open in India, paid previews set Witnessing strong early interest from fans, the advance bookings for The Devil Wears Prada 2 have started in India. The sequel starring Meryl Streep and Anne Hathaway will also have special paid previews.

13 years of Aashiqui 2: Aditya-Shraddha and Mohit Suri revisit memories Actors Aditya Roy Kapur, Shraddha Kapoor and filmmaker Mohit Suri revisited Aashiqui 2 memories on its 13th anniversary. Their posts renewed attention to a film that remains one of Bollywood's most loved romantic musicals.

Karan Johar slams Bollywood PR overdrive, says work should speak itself Filmmaker Karan Johar has slammed Bollywood's growing dependence on paid publicity and performative film promotion. He said the trend makes it harder to know whether audience approval is genuine or purchased.

Rihanna performs puja, plays Phoolon Ki Holi at Ambani residence in Mumbai. Video Rihanna visited Antilia during her Mumbai trip and joined the Ambani family for puja and phoolon ki holi. She is in India for the launch of her cosmetics brand, Fenty Beauty.

Sonu Nigam surprises fans playing his song in their car ahead of Rotterdam concert Sonu Nigam shared a video of his fans in a nearby car playing Yeh Ishq Ishq as they drove to his Rotterdam concert and surprised them with a wave.
Ek Din: Aamir Khan on unusual 39-day early booking strategy for Sai-Junaid's film Aamir Khan opened up on the early advance booking strategy for Sai Pallavi and Junaid Khan's romantic drama, Ek Din.

How Michael Jackson turned a 2-min pause into Super Bowl history An older video of Michael Jackson has surfaced online, showing him pausing the start of his Super Bowl halftime show for nearly two minutes.

Rhea Chakraborty's frozen bank accounts released in Sushant Singh Rajput case A Mumbai special court allowed the unfreezing of four bank accounts linked to actor Rhea Chakraborty, her brother Showik and mother Sandhya. The court said that the NCB had not complied with Section 68F of the NDPS Act within the required period.

Akshay Kumar's fun run is on: Hera Pheri to Bhooth Bangla, Golmaal 5 and beyond Bhooth Bangla reiterates that Akshay Kumar is the only superstar in Bollywood who knows how to tap mass-market comedy for box office numbers. It's simple: Promise them a jolly good laugh, they'll come.

Critics are morons: Farah Khan demands Oscar for Jaafar Jackson in Michael Farah Khan shared a nostalgic picture with late pop icon Michael Jackson on social media. She backed Michael lead actor Jaafar Jackson for an Oscar.

Can Vijay turn borrowed MGR symbolism into real political power? Vijay invoked MGR at a TVK conference as his party entered its first Assembly election. The comparison highlights how closely Tamil cinema, symbolism and political credibility remain tied in the state.

Bunny meets Avi: Ranbir–Aditya reunion sparks Yeh Jawaani Hai Deewani nostalgia Actors Ranbir Kapoor and Aditya Roy Kapur were seen together in Mumbai, much to the delight of Yeh Jawaani Hai Deewani fans. The sighting revived memories of their characters, Bunny and Avi, as the film continues to enjoy strong fan love.

Wheel of Fortune finale: Akshay Kumar gets teary-eyed on stage after tribute A tribute video during the Wheel of Fortune India finale shoot left Akshay Kumar visibly emotional. The actor reflected on the show's journey and thanked viewers for their support.

Viral video: Rihanna learns Bharatanatyam mudras during Mumbai visit International pop star Rihanna tried mastering Bharatanatyam during her Mumbai visit. A video of her learning mudras and expressions of the classical dance form is now being widely shared online.

general news.headline: If I were Iran agent...: US gala shooter's chilling manifesto before opening fireMinutes before the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting, Cole Tomas Allen allegedly sent his family a manifesto outlining targets and methods. The document, now part of the investigation, also claimed security at the venue was easy to bypass.

Amid fish ban row, PM offers prayers at Kolkata Kalibari known for non-veg prasadNarendra Modi offered prayers at Kolkata's Thanthania Kalibari before his roadshow. The visit drew attention to the 300-year-old shrine's rare non-vegetarian prasad tradition and Ramakrishna link.

Shots fired, guests duck, Vance whisked away: A blow-by-blow of press gala chaosA gunman opened fire outside the White House press dinner and was taken into custody. The breach triggered panic, evacuations and a sweeping security lockdown at the venue.

US press gala shooting suspect wrote manifesto before attack: Read full textCole Tomas Allen was arrested after gunfire disrupted the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington. Investigators say he intended to target Trump administration officials, including President Donald Trump.

Rihanna performs puja, plays Phoolon Ki Holi at Ambani residence in Mumbai. VideoRihanna visited Antilia during her Mumbai trip and joined the Ambani family for puja and phoolon ki holi. She is in India for the launch of her cosmetics brand, Fenty Beauty.

Pakistan not a suitable intermediary, says Iranian MP amid stalled talksIranian lawmaker Ebrahim Rezaei said Pakistan is not a suitable intermediary in the ongoing talks. His remarks come as Abbas Araghchi steps up diplomacy from Oman to Islamabad to keep negotiations moving.

If they want to talk…: Trump sets conditions for Iran as Araghchi returns to PakEfforts to revive formal negotiations have suffered a setback after Donald Trump cancelled a planned visit by US envoys to Pakistan.

Seven Missed Calls – Inside story of how AAP lost its Upper House in seven daysA covert political operation saw seven AAP Rajya Sabha MPs defect, blindsiding Arvind Kejriwal and strengthening BJP's numbers in Parliament, shifting the balance in the Upper House.

The Raga of Raghu RaiWith Raghu Rai's passing, India has lost not just a legendary photographer, but one of its most sensitive chroniclers. Through five decades of unforgettable images, he gave the nation a face, a memory, and a mirror.

Rs 289 crore, 25 acres, 15 students: Inside India's 'ghost campus'Handcrafted ByOur Editors Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya Institute of Archaeology in Greater Noida is built to be one of India's premier centres for training and research in archaeology, especially for educational purposes. Spread across 25 acres and built with an investment of Rs 289 crore, the campus was inaugurated by Prime Minister Modi in 2019. But when I visited the campus on a weekday, what I found raised more questions than answers.

Pawan Khera vs Assam CM's wife in Supreme Court after HC denies pre-arrest bailCongress leader Pawan Khera has approached the Supreme Court after being denied anticipatory bail, seeking relief in a case linked to his remarks against Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma's wife.

Your traditional domestic help vs app-based house help, which one would you choose?Can hiring an app-based house help become the new normal? We spoke to several families as well as domestic workers in and around Delhi-NCR and beyond. What we found out was very interesting.

Selena Gomez-Benny Blanco to divorce? Here's what we know about the viral claimViral posts have claimed Selena Gomez and Benny Blanco are heading for a divorce. Read on to know what led to the speculation about the couple, who got married on September 27, 2025.

Only matter of time: Bombshell report claims FBI chief Kash Patel to be fired soonA report says Donald Trump is likely to back the removal of FBI director Kash Patel after weeks of damaging coverage and allegations, including his alleged excessive drinking habit.

Delhi to boil at 45°C on Monday, Northeast set for stormy soakingIndia braces for a sharply split weather day on Monday, April 27, with heatwave conditions roasting Delhi, Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan, Bihar and Gujarat at 43 to 45 degrees Celsius.

Gwalior woman gets divorce by falsely naming sister-in-law as husband's second wifeA Gwalior woman allegedly secured an ex-parte divorce by identifying her husband's sister as his second wife. The husband has challenged the decree in the High Court, alleging that the court was misled.

Meet Robin Singh, the Bhojpuri commentator who is giving Bihar cricket its voiceFrom dusty fields to floodlit stadiums, Bihar's cricketing voice is finally being heard. Robin Singh, a coach-turned Bhojpuri commentator, who first built cricketers in silence and now tells their stories to millions.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Ahmedabad customs seize 61 designer pens worth up to Rs 30 lakhCustoms officials at Ahmedabad airport seized 61 premium designer pens from a passenger arriving from Japan via Vietnam. The haul, valued at up to Rs 30 lakh, has triggered questions over duty evasion and possible smuggling links.

Irked by loud partying, drunk Delhi cop shoots delivery boy, injures anotherNeeraj, the accused constable, got into a vigorous argument with Pandav, the victim, last night. As the heated exchange of words escalated, he took out his gun and shot him.

Why Indians are spending up to Rs 50,000 to run on JUST ONE DAYThousands of runners are spending heavily on travelling and participating in marathons in India like the TCS World 10K Bengaluru, and Wipro Bengaluru Marathon. Marathons have mushroomed and the number of participants has grown manifold. A simple sport like running has become a part of corporate India's fitness ritual.

Gunman likely targeted Trump, officials; stayed in hotel hosting White House eventA gunman opened fire at a security checkpoint during the White House Correspondents' Association dinner in Washington. Investigators said President Donald Trump and other administration officials were likely the intended targets.

Himalayan snow hits 23-year low, water crisis looms for 2 billion livesA new report has found that snow cover across the Hindu Kush Himalaya plunged 27.8 per cent below average this winter, the lowest in more than two decades.

Do terrorists live in Bengal? Kejriwal jabs BJP over central forces' deploymentCampaigning for the TMC candidate in Kolkata, Arvind Kejriwal questioned why the BJP had sent paramilitary forces to Bengal and framed the election as a battle to protect democracy and Bengali identity.

sports.headline: Criminal: Dale Steyn blasts LSG for sending Nicholas Pooran in Super Over vs KKR IPL 2026, LSG vs KKR: Former South Africa speedster Dale Steyn said Lucknow Super Giants should not have used Nicholas Pooran in the Super Over against Kolkata Knight Riders, given his poor run of form.

Definitely need a break: Rishabh Pant's reaction after Super Over loss vs KKR LSG vs KKR: Rishabh Pant said Lucknow Super Giants need a break, a reset and greater accountability after their crushing Super Over defeat to Kolkata Knight Riders left them bottom of the IPL table.

Rinku Singh rescues KKR again as LSG hit rock bottom after Super Over thriller IPL 2026, LSG vs KKR: Rinku Singh scored 83 runs, took five catches and hit the winning runs in the Super Over as Kolkata Knight Riders beat the Lucknow Super Giants at the Ekana Stadium in Lucknow.

Kartik Tyagi allowed to bowl despite 2 waist-high full tosses: Explained Kartik Tyagi bowled 2 waist-high full tosses in the final over of Lucknow vs Kolkata. However, he was allowed to continue with his over. Here's why?

Shubman Gill unhappy with Gujarat Titans despite big win vs CSK Gujarat Titans captain Shubman Gill was not happy with his team despite a big win against Chennai Super Kings. Gill said that the team needed to close out innings better with the ball.

Self-belief, small tweaks: How Deepti Sharma bounced back to silence critics SAW vs INDW, 4th T20I: Deepti Sharma bounced back to form and silenced her critics, finishing with figures of 4-0-19-5 to help India beat South Africa by 14 runs on Saturday in Johannesburg.

MS Dhoni injured again? CSK head coach explains why he did not play vs GT IPL 2026: Stephen Fleming highlighted that MS Dhoni's calf injury resurfaced during their warm-up game. CSK are looking to play it safe so that the injury does not rule him out of the season.

Unpredictable Chepauk pitch digs CSK's grave in Chennai Chennai Super Kings faltered at home against Gujarat Titans in a day-game on Sunday, April 26. An unpredictable pitch turned out to be the downfall for CSK, who simply did not score enough runs against Shubman Gill's Gujarat.

Frustrated CSK fans leave Chepauk early after poor show during clash against GT IPL 2026, CSK vs GT: Chennai Super Kings slumped to an eight-wicket defeat against Gujarat Titans at Chepauk, with frustrated fans leaving early as Sai Sudharsan's blistering knock sealed the chase.

Angkrish Raghuvanshi run-out controversy: Was it out or not out? LSG vs KKR: Kolkata Knight Riders were left fuming after the TV umpire gave Angkrish Raghuvanshi run-out for obstructing the field. KKR coaches Shane Watson and Abhishek Nayar were seen arguing with the umpire after their batter was given out for obstructing the field.

Mayank Yadav return to IPL lasts one game. Pacer dropped after conceding 60 runs LSG vs KKR, IPL 2026: Mayank Yadav was benched by the Lucknow Super Giants despite the pacer making his return to the side after a long injury spell.

LSG vs KKR Highlights: Rinku shines in Lucknow as Kolkata win Super OverLucknow (LSG) vs Kolkata (KKR) Highlights, IPL 2026: Rinku Singh completes the job for Kolkata Knight Riders as he hits the boundary to wrap up this game. KKR win the Super Over.

Indian badminton legend's response to new scoring system: Had feared for worseBadminton as we know, is set to change after the world federation approved a change in the scoring system. From 3 games of 21 points, it's shifting to 3 games of 15. Indian badminton legend Pullela Gopichand had issued a warning about the same a few months back.

Gujarat Titans or Pakistan? Rashid Khan, Manav Suthar make comical error vs CSKIPL 2026, CSK vs GT: Rashid Khan and Manav Suthar made a comical error at the Chepauk on Sunday, gifting Kartik Sharma a boundary, echoing Pakistan's infamous 2008 fielding blunder against the West Indies.

Kenya's Sabastian Sawe creates marathon history with first sub-two-hour runSabastian Sawe shattered the marathon world record in London, becoming the first man to run a legal sub-two-hour marathon with a stunning 1:59:30 finish on a historic day.

Ashwin reveals Vaibhav Sooryavanshi mania at home: My parents are his No.1 fansR Ashwin said Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's surge has made his parents the Rajasthan Royals opener's biggest fans. He said the batter's appeal lies in how strongly he resembles India's street-cricket dream.

Ishowspeed sends shoutout to Virat Kohli, trolls Babar Azam in viral videoYouTuber IShowSpeed went viral after praising Virat Kohli and taking a playful swipe at Babar Azam while playing cricket in Trinidad during his latest trip. Speed has been known to be an ardent fan of Kohli.

Why is MS Dhoni not playing? CSK fans enraged as Thala misses 8th IPL 2026 gameMS Dhoni missed his eighth game of IPL 2026 as Chennai Super Kings faced Gujarat Titans at Chepauk. The lack of any clear update from the franchise or captain Ruturaj Gaikwad fuelled fan anger and retirement speculation.

A dangerous virus that doesn't catch spreads in IPL 2026IPL 2026: The catching efficiency in the Indian Premier League has consistently gone down since 2020. It seems to have hit rock bottom in IPL 2026 where, every single day, several important chances are going down every single game. The question is, in the age of hyper-athleticism, why are so many catches being dropped in this high-profile tournament?

Trust in Shreyas Iyer influenced PBKS IPL auction calls: Coach Ricky PontingIPL 2025: Ricky Ponting said Shreyas Iyer justified Punjab Kings' big auction push with a match-winning unbeaten 71 in the record chase against Delhi Capitals. Calm under pressure, he turned a daunting chase into a statement win.

I know your secrets, go easy on my team: Kishan's funny chat with SooryavanshiIshan Kishan said he told Vaibhav Sooryavanshi jokingly to go easy on Sunrisers Hyderabad because he knew all his secrets. Sooryavanshi scored his second IPL hundred against Hyderabad during the match on April 25.

Meet Robin Singh, the Bhojpuri commentator who is giving Bihar cricket its voiceFrom dusty fields to floodlit stadiums, Bihar's cricketing voice is finally being heard. Robin Singh, a coach-turned Bhojpuri commentator, who first built cricketers in silence and now tells their stories to millions.

Swiatek retires ill in Madrid as Ann Li reaches last 16Iga Swiatek retired from her Madrid Open third-round match against Ann Li after falling ill in the decider. The stomach virus ended the defending champion's run and sent Li into the last 16.

RR need to think if Riyan is right captain: Sehwag's brutal take after SRH lossVirender Sehwag questioned Riyan Parag's captaincy after RR failed to defend 228 against SRH. He said the tactical calls and Parag's batting slump now leave the management with a decision to make.

Avinash Sable may skip Commonwealth Games if not fully fitAvinash Sable has said he may miss the 2026 Commonwealth Games as he recovers from a serious knee injury. His decision will depend on whether he can regain full fitness rather than merely qualify.

Has Mohamed Salah played his last game for Liverpool? Arne Slot reactsArne Slot says it is too early to know if Mohamed Salah has played his final Liverpool match after the Egyptian limped off with a hamstring injury against Crystal Palace. Salah will leave Liverpool at the end of the season.

Give Vaibhav Sooryavanshi Lara treatment before India Debut: BrathwaiteCarlos Braithwaite has urged India not to rush Vaibhav Sooryavanshi into international cricket despite his blazing IPL form. Braithwaite said the young batter would benefit from spending time around senior players before making his international bow.

India bounce back with dominant 3-0 win over Ukraine in Uber Cup FinalsIndia's women bounced back from their opening defeat by beating Ukraine 3-0 in Group A at the Uber Cup Finals. The result keeps their quarter-final hopes alive ahead of a decisive meeting with China.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi injured? RR coach provides positive update after SRH scareIPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi walked off with a hamstring problem during Rajasthan Royals' match against Sunrisers Hyderabad. Vikram Rathour later said the issue did not appear serious, easing immediate concern for Rajasthan.

Chennai keep waiting on MS Dhoni as Gujarat mount comeback bidIPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings and Gujarat Titans will face off at Chepauk in the first match of Sunday's doubleheader, followed by the clash between Kolkata Knight Riders and Lucknow Super Giants in Lucknow.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
